question: Can hierarchical multi-agent topologies with automatic delegation outperform flat architectures on complex software engineering tasks?
hypothesis: Hierarchical topologies will achieve higher quality scores on complex tasks but with higher latency and cost.

variables.independent: agent topology (hierarchical vs flat vs swarm)
variables.controlled: same LLM model, same task set, same evaluation rubric

risks: small sample size may reduce statistical power

question: How does retrieval-augmented generation with agentic query refinement compare to naive RAG on ambiguous user queries?
hypothesis: Agentic RAG with query refinement will resolve ambiguity in 70%+ of cases where naive RAG fails.

variables.independent: RAG approach (naive vs agentic with refinement)
variables.controlled: same corpus, same embedding model, same LLM

expected_datasets: custom ambiguous queries from support tickets
risks: refinement loop may increase latency beyond acceptable threshold

question: What is the minimum viable memory system for an agent to maintain coherent multi-session conversations?
hypothesis: A lightweight episodic memory with semantic search is sufficient for 80% of multi-session coherence, without the overhead of a full knowledge graph.

variables.independent: memory system complexity (none vs episodic vs episodic+semantic vs full KG)
variables.controlled: same agent, same conversation topics, same evaluation criteria

risks: KG construction overhead may not justify marginal quality gain
